Token Standard:   ERC-3643 compliant token on Mantle Network
Token Symbol:     HIBT
Face Value:       USD 1,000 per token
Total Issuance:   USD 100,000,000 (100,000 tokens)
Coupon Rate:      5.50% per annum (semi-annual coupon payment)
Coupon Payment:   15 January and 15 July each year
Maturity Date:    15 July 2031 (5-year tenor)
Settlement:       T+2 via on-chain atomic settlement
Minimum Subscription: USD 200,000 (200 tokens)

Use of Proceeds:
  вЂ” 60% allocated to Asia logistics infrastructure projects
  вЂ” 25% allocated to cross-border supply chain digitalisation
  вЂ” 15% allocated to working capital and issuance costs

Each token represents a legal, direct, unconditional and unsubordinated obligation
of the Issuer. Ownership is recorded permanently on Mantle Network blockchain.
The trust deed is registered with and held in custody by the trustee.

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ

This offering is exclusively available to Professional Investors (PI) and
institutional investors as defined under Schedule 1 of the Securities and
Futures Ordinance. Accredited investors from FATF-member jurisdictions may
subscribe subject to applicable local regulations.

Minimum net assets requirement: HKD 8,000,000 or equivalent for individual PI.
Institutional PI: SFC-licensed firms, insurance companies, banks, MPF trustees.

Transfer restrictions are enforced on-chain: tokens may only be transferred to
KYC-verified, whitelisted investor addresses. The ERC-3643 compliance module
performs eligibility checks on every transfer, preventing distribution to
non-qualifying investors. On-chain enforcement is automatic and tamper-proof.

AML / KYC PROCEDURES
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ

The Issuer has implemented full AMLO (Cap. 615)-compliant KYC procedures.
All investors undergo identity verification including:
  вЂ” Government-issued ID document verification
  вЂ” Proof of address (utility bill or bank statement dated within 3 months)
  вЂ” Source of funds declaration for subscriptions exceeding USD 500,000
  вЂ” PEP (Politically Exposed Person) and sanctions screening via Refinitiv World-Check
  вЂ” Ongoing transaction monitoring post-issuance

KYC records are maintained for a minimum of 7 years per AMLO requirements.
AI-assisted identity verification is performed via the HarbourRWA IdentityRegistry
smart contract on Mantle Network. Each investor wallet is whitelisted only after
full due diligence completion.

RISK FACTORS & DISCLOSURE
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ

Investors should carefully consider the following material risk factors:

1. Credit Risk: Risk of issuer default on coupon or principal obligations.
   Mitigated by A2/A credit rating and USD 350M in tangible asset backing.

2. Interest Rate Risk: Bond market value is inversely correlated with prevailing
   interest rates. Mark-to-market losses may occur during rising rate environments.
   Hedging via SOFR swap arrangements are in place.

3. Regulatory Risk: Changes to SFC regulations governing tokenised securities,
   including potential reclassification of digital tokens, may affect the legal
   structure, transferability, and secondary market liquidity of this instrument.

4. Technology Risk: Smart contract vulnerabilities may expose investors to loss.
   All HarbourRWA smart contracts have been independently audited by PeckShield Inc.
   with zero critical or high-severity findings prior to deployment on Mantle Mainnet.

5. Liquidity Risk: No guaranteed secondary market currently exists for HIBT tokens.
   Redemption is available at par value at maturity only. A secondary trading
   facility may be established on an SFC-licensed exchange at a future date.

6. Concentration Risk: Revenue dependency on Asia infrastructure sector.
   Diversification across sub-sectors mitigates sector-specific downturns.

7. Custody Risk: Loss of private keys or custodian insolvency could affect access
   to token holdings. Mitigated by institutional custodian with segregated accounts.

Valuation Methodology:
The bond is valued at par (USD 1,000 per token) plus accrued interest calculated
on an Actual/365 day-count convention. Independent valuation by Bloomberg BVAL
is performed monthly and made available to all investors via the investor portal.

Redemption Terms:
  вЂ” Full principal redemption at maturity: 15 July 2031
  вЂ” Optional early issuer call: from 15 July 2029 with 30-day notice
  вЂ” Early redemption price: par plus make-whole premium (Treasury + 25 bps)
  вЂ” No investor put option unless material adverse change event occurs

Fees & Charges:
  вЂ” Subscription fee: 0.25% of subscription amount
  вЂ” Annual management fee: 0.08% per annum on outstanding balance
  вЂ” Redemption fee: Nil at maturity; make-whole + 0.20% for early issuer call
  вЂ” Custody fee: 0.05% per annum (charged to investor accounts quarterly)

Conflict of Interest:
Harbour Capital Markets Corporation Limited acts as both issuer and placement agent
for this offering. The potential conflict of interest has been disclosed to and
acknowledged by all subscribing investors in accordance with SFC Code of Conduct
para. 13.5.

Investors should carefully consider the following material risk factors:

1. Credit Risk: Risk of issuer default on coupon or principal obligations.
   Mitigated by A2/A credit rating and USD 350M in tangible asset backing.

2. Interest Rate Risk: Bond market value is inversely correlated with prevailing
   interest rates. Mark-to-market losses may occur during rising rate environments.
   Hedging via SOFR swap arrangements are in place.

3. Regulatory Risk: Changes to SFC regulations governing tokenised securities,
   including potential reclassification of digital tokens, may affect the legal
   structure, transferability, and secondary market liquidity of this instrument.

4. Technology Risk: Smart contract vulnerabilities may expose investors to loss.
   All HarbourRWA smart contracts have been independently audited by PeckShield Inc.
   with zero critical or high-severity findings prior to deployment on Mantle Mainnet.

5. Liquidity Risk: No guaranteed secondary market currently exists for HIBT tokens.
   Redemption is available at par value at maturity only. A secondary trading
   facility may be established on an SFC-licensed exchange at a future date.

6. Concentration Risk: Revenue dependency on Asia infrastructure sector.
   Diversification across sub-sectors mitigates sector-specific downturns.

7. Custody Risk: Loss of private keys or custodian insolvency could affect access
   to token holdings. Mitigated by institutional custodian with segregated accounts.
